recommended_next_actions: Review the latest imported reused-FA-session artifacts from April 3, 2026 and continue from the now-active upstream session-origin seam rather than reopening route ownership, parity, bridge identification, or generic UserSession work.

The AFF session route, the UserSession bridge behind it, and the reused FA-session chain are now all bounded: Apache `/FireFlow/api/session` and the direct local aff-boot session path still match on HTTP 200 plus `INVALID_SESSION_KEY`, and the imported retained FireFlow proof now shows concrete reuse pairs such as `e0974e9f0e -> n575jpek15` repeating seven times plus additional reuse of `0o43kjh144` across several FireFlow session ids.

The strongest next ambiguity is now `trace_businessflow_session_origin`: stay just upstream of the confirmed reused FA-session chain and decide which BusinessFlow or AFA-side caller fed or refreshed that session context before FireFlow reused it.

A second strong seam is `inspect_java_runtime_clusters`, starting from `algosec-ms`, `ms-metro`, `ms-bflow`, and `aff-boot`, because the current proof now separates several concrete local listener owners without yet explaining the full Java cluster boundaries.
Keep using the remote-owned runtime flow: adf-dev at 10.167.2.151 remains the runtime and support-facing host, while 10.167.2.150 remains the runtime evidence source.

Treat the current lab posture as exploration-friendly: bounded misconfiguration or temporary breakage on 10.167.2.150 is acceptable because the lab is quickly redeployable and later distributed-architecture labs are expected.

If promotion-compatible remote proof later matters for this pack, record and address the missed fresh-pack rehearsal step separately rather than pretending the current long-lived pack already has that certification.

required_gates.summary: Confirm the fresh successor template remains PackFactory-valid and can materialize a first proving-ground build-pack without broadening the testing surface.
